sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# First verify we're back to initial state
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
def get_color(r, c):
    for n in seg:
        if n['pixels'] == 4:
            b = n['boundary']
            min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
            min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
            if min_r == r and min_c == c:
                return n['color']
    return '?'

left_ring = [
    (17, 23), (17, 26), (17, 29), (17, 32), (17, 35),
    (20, 35), (23, 35), (26, 35), (29, 35), (32, 35), (35, 35), (38, 35), (41, 35),
    (44, 35), (44, 32), (44, 29), (44, 26), (44, 23),
    (41, 23), (38, 23), (35, 23), (32, 23), (29, 23), (26, 23), (23, 23), (20, 23),
]
ring_colors = [get_color(r, c) for r, c in left_ring]
print(f"Current ring: {' '.join(ring_colors)}")
print(f"Expected init: S b p Y p b S g w g p w b g p p S S g Y b w w b g p")
print(f"Match: {ring_colors == ['S','b','p','Y','p','b','S','g','w','g','p','w','b','g','p','p','S','S','g','Y','b','w','w','b','g','p']}")

# Yellow markers bracket positions (26,35) and (35,35)
# Ring positions: 7=(26,35), 11=(35,35)
# Y blocks at positions 3 and 19
# To get Y at position 7: shift CW by 4 (pos 3 → pos 7)
# After 4 CW: Y at pos 7, other Y at pos 15

# Try shifting ring CW 4 times to get Y at (26,35)
print("\nShifting ring CW 4 times...")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 20}] * 4)

seg2 = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
def get_color2(r, c):
    for n in seg2:
        if n['pixels'] == 4:
            b = n['boundary']
            min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
            min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
            if min_r == r and min_c == c:
                return n['color']
    return '?'

ring_colors2 = [get_color2(r, c) for r, c in left_ring]
print(f"Ring after 4 CW: {' '.join(ring_colors2)}")
print(f"Y at pos 7 (26,35): {get_color2(26, 35)}")
print(f"Y at pos 11 (35,35): {get_color2(35, 35)}")
print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
